question.text: A small-cap equity fund would most likely be placed at which level of the Risk-o-meter?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Small-cap equity funds invest in small company stocks which can go up or down significantly. This makes them among the riskiest mutual fund categories, placing them at "Very High" on the Risk-o-meter.
**The Trap:** Just because a fund is "Very High" risk does not mean it will always lose money. It means the value can fluctuate a lot — both upward and downward.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in Series V-A. Understanding which types of funds fall where on the Risk-o-meter is important.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Small-cap funds suit investors with a long time horizon (5+ years) and high risk tolerance. They are not appropriate for someone who needs money in 1-2 years.
